title: The Adventure Begins
logline: Keen sails away from home to prove he is ready, loses himself in a storm, and discovers that courage is love carried into the big water when someone else needs him.
declaration: This episode is about a child discovering that growing up does not mean sailing away from love — it means carrying that love into the big water and choosing anyway. When it ends, the child watching should feel brave-in-their-tummy, like the world is bigger than they thought but they can move through it with help; the parent beside them should feel the ache and pride of letting a child go, knowing protection sometimes becomes trust. The scene that carries the most weight is Scene 7, the underwater rescue, because Keen is lost, frightened, and nearly out of strength, yet he chooses Squeaky before he knows he can succeed — and the wristbands glow not as a prize, but as love remembered at the exact moment he needs it. The colour of this episode is storm-blue turning aquamarine: cold uncertainty warmed from inside. The moment I am most proud of is Keen seeing his father’s wristbands underwater and finding one more pull — because it trusts the child to feel something real: courage can be shaky, wet, tired, and still true.

theme.declaration: This episode is about a child discovering that growing up does not mean sailing away from love — it means carrying that love into the big water and choosing anyway. When it ends, the child watching should feel brave-in-their-tummy, like the world is bigger than they thought but they can move through it with help; the parent beside them should feel the ache and pride of letting a child go, knowing protection sometimes becomes trust. The scene that carries the most weight is Scene 7, the underwater rescue, because Keen is lost, frightened, and nearly out of strength, yet he chooses Squeaky before he knows he can succeed — and the wristbands glow not as a prize, but as love remembered at the exact moment he needs it. The colour of this episode is storm-blue turning aquamarine: cold uncertainty warmed from inside. The moment I am most proud of is Keen seeing his father’s wristbands underwater and finding one more pull — because it trusts the child to feel something real: courage can be shaky, wet, tired, and still true.
theme.theme: Growing up means learning that love does not keep you from the big water — it travels with you, so you can choose courage when the world feels too large.
theme.leadArc: Keen begins wanting to prove he is ready, grown-up, and brave enough to sail alone; underneath, he needs to discover that courage is not being unafraid or unsupported, but admitting the bigness of the world, receiving the love he carries from home, and acting through the fear for someone else.
theme.storySpine: Once upon a time, in Crystal Cove and beyond it, there was a young bear named Keen who wanted to prove he was ready for his first great journey. Every day, he tried to look capable, checking every strap and saying he had things under control. Until one day, his Mum gave him his father’s wristbands and let him sail away into open water. Because of that, Keen met Squeaky, lost his map, and found himself in a storm much bigger than his confidence. Because of that, when Squeaky became trapped, Keen had to choose between staying safe and diving into the frightening water to help. Until finally, with the love of his family glowing through the wristbands and the Crystal Bears calling from shore, Keen pulled Squeaky free and came home to a new family. And ever since then, Keen has known that being brave is not sailing alone — it is carrying love with you and choosing what matters.
theme.promise: By the final frame, the audience must feel that Keen belongs: the storm has passed, the ache of leaving home still matters, but the cove is warmer because he has found another circle of love without losing the first.
theme.throughline: The tonal spine is joy with a saltwater ache: Fuzzby’s ridiculous pollen chaos and honey obsession keep the episode buoyant, while Keen’s goodbye, the lost boat, and the father’s wristbands keep reminding us that every adventure costs something. The bittersweet lives in the fact that Mum has to let him go, Keen has to feel lost before he can be found, and the new family does not replace the old one — it makes the distance survivable.
theme.selCompetency: Courage — Keen’s Aquamarine lesson is responsible brave choice-making: he learns that true courage includes the gulp, the uncertainty, the need for help, and the decision to act for someone else anyway.
theme.haidt: For the parent, the episode quietly speaks to the modern fear of letting children face real-world risk. It argues for adventurous independence inside a web of connection: not bubble-wrapping, not abandonment, but allowing the child to meet challenge, build competence, receive community, and discover that they are capable of more than constant protection would let them know.
theme.pressureTest: Yes, the truth is universal and earnable here because the script gives Keen a real departure, a real storm, a real choice, and a real rescue before he receives the crystal. The danger is making courage look like instant heroism, a shiny reward, or a moral announced by adults. The trap every scene must avoid is smoothing away the fear: if Keen seems fearless, the episode becomes false; if Aida or the bears explain the lesson too neatly, it becomes preachy; if the ending becomes pure sweetness, it betrays the goodbye. We must keep the gulp, the lostness, the mother’s tear, the underwater exhaustion, and the remembered love inside the victory.

beatRule: one 10-12s Seedance take per beat — the beat directs its own internal cuts (director skill v5.0)
_note: Authored by cb_director (Gate 1, BEAT-NATIVE) from the script — 43 beats, 10 scenes.
style: Premium 3D animated feature film aesthetic for children aged 4 to 8, bright hyper saturated colours, warm golden hour sunlight with volumetric rays, glowing magical particles, lighthearted highly expressive slapstick comedy

scenes.name: Rainforest Pollen Run
scenes.location: A bee-height wildflower corridor inside the Crystal Cove rainforest, packed with towering pollen-heavy blossoms, broad leaves, one low clipping branch, and a narrow sky gap beyond the deeper trees.
scenes.time: Morning, early in the same warm day.
scenes.weather: Clear and breezy, with the first distant pressure change not yet visible until the thunder cue.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden morning light with pollen glitter in the air; by the final beat the warmth cools slightly at the edges as storm pressure arrives.
scenes.look: Empty establishing plate with giant pollen flowers dominating foreground, midground, and background; open blossoms form a left-to-right flight path with several deep petal cups for dives and stuck-in-flower gags, a springy broad leaf sits mid-flight for the FWIP ricochet, and a thin branch crosses the forward path before another open blossom. Warm pollen motes drift through the air, petals and stems sway in a clear breeze, and the far treeline frames a small upper sky gap where the light is just beginning to cool for the later thunder cue. No characters, no props, nothing outside the pollen-run action space.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene lets the child laugh before the storm and plants the first feeling that the world can change suddenly, so Keen’s later big water already has a comic, living world around it.
scenes.sceneShotName: Rainforest Pollen Meadow — warm morning tall-flower corridor, bee's-eye wide
scenes.definingFeature: a dense corridor of colossal sunlit pollen-flowers with one springy leaf and one low branch built into the bee flight path
scenes.colorTemperature: warm amber morning safety with a faint cool distant edge
scenes.cameraHeight: low bee's-eye looking slightly up through the towering flowers
scenes.ambientBed: A warm rainforest meadow bed: light breeze through tall flowers, distant birdsong, a soft steady hum of insect life.
scenes.parentLine: Under the slapstick, a parent watches a confident kid perform competence to earn approval, then get loved for the mess underneath instead — and sees the storm arrive exactly when that armor is down.
scenes.sceneLook: Warm golden morning light through the flower-meadow corridor, pollen glittering and drifting in the air, towering blossoms and stems on every side.

scenes.name: Aida's Sanctuary Vision
scenes.location: A sheltered Crystal Cove shoreline sanctuary where the forest meets bright morning water, with a small ritual space beside the waves and a clear path back into the trees.
scenes.time: Morning, shortly after the rainforest opening.
scenes.weather: Clear coastal breeze with distant wind beginning to gather far offshore.
scenes.lighting: Soft warm morning gold over blue water, shaped by rose-pink crystal glow during Aida’s vision and settling back into natural cove light.
scenes.look: The empty plate is built around the crystal bowl set near the shoreline, wand resting at the rim, with gentle waves rolling in behind it and the forest path opening screen-right for Aida’s later turn toward the woods. Warm morning light crosses the sand and low coastal plants; the cove feels held and watchful, with only a faint suggestion of distant wind gathering far offshore. No characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: Aida feels the moment a child is about to step beyond home, and the audience should feel that love is already watching before Keen knows he will need it.
scenes.sceneShotName: Aida's cove sanctuary — morning crystal bowl by shore, medium-wide
scenes.definingFeature: the crystal bowl at the water’s edge, poised between the quiet cove and the forest path
scenes.colorTemperature: warm golden morning with rose-quartz tenderness
scenes.cameraHeight: eye-level, slightly lowered toward the bowl
scenes.ambientBed: Gentle cove waves lapping the shore under a soft, low ambient shimmer, distant wind threading through
scenes.parentLine: A parent recognizes the quiet dread of watching a child prepare to leave before he even knows he'll need the strength she's already sending him.
scenes.sceneLook: Soft warm morning gold over blue water, shaped by rose-pink crystal glow during Aida's vision and settling back into natural cove light.

scenes.name: The Pier Goodbye
scenes.location: A weathered wooden pier at Crystal Cove, with the homeward dock and shoreline screen-left and calm open water leading screen-right toward the horizon.
scenes.time: Late morning, continuing the same clear day.
scenes.weather: Fair and breezy, with calm water near the dock and only distant hints of change.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden morning light, bright blue-water bounce and soft highlights that make the goodbye feel beautiful but fragile.
scenes.look: Empty late-morning goodbye plate: the tied little sailboat bobs beside the dock, its mooring rope looped to a pier post; the rolled map, satchel, berry pouch and blanket are arranged in and beside the boat for final packing. A small keepsake bag rests near the pier edge, with simple rope rails and dock posts framing the departure path. Calm water fills the screen-right half of the frame, wide enough for the boat to push off, wobble, drift away and for Squeaky to splash beside it; the pier remains readable behind as the place love lets go from. No characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: Keen wants to look ready while his Mum has to let him go, and the audience must feel the proud ache of love becoming trust as the vacant cuffs pass into his paws.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove pier — late-morning goodbye, moored sailboat wide
scenes.definingFeature: the little sailboat moored at the edge of the pier, pointed toward the big open water
scenes.colorTemperature: warm amber late-morning safety with cool sea-blue distance
scenes.cameraHeight: low dock-level, slightly looking out toward screen-right open water
scenes.ambientBed: Pier water lapping softly against the dock pilings, a light coastal breeze, distant gulls
scenes.parentLine: A parent recognises the whole architecture of letting a child go: the over-preparation, the borrowed courage, the heirloom passed at last, and the promise that love opens its hands instead of holding on.
scenes.sceneLook: Warm golden morning light, bright blue-water bounce and soft highlights that make the goodbye feel beautiful but fragile.

scenes.name: Open Water Storm
scenes.location: Endless open water beyond Crystal Cove, a small sailboat route crossing screen-right into a vast sea where the horizon is being swallowed by an incoming storm front.
scenes.weather: Clear open water turns quickly into gusts, heavy rain, lightning in the distance and roughening waves.
scenes.lighting: Bright blue daylight drains into cold grey-blue storm light; visibility collapses behind rain sheets.
scenes.look: A wide empty plate dominated by rolling open water and a huge darkening sky; the little sailboat sits low in frame with mast, sail, tiller and wet deck visible but no characters aboard, pointed screen-right into open sea. Clearer blue water remains at frame-left while grey rough water, rain curtains and distant lightning gather frame-right, leaving enough sea surface beside the boat for a dolphin to surface, circle, leap and surge ahead. No land, no extra boats, no decorative objects — only water, sky, storm, and the boat the scene requires.
scenes.emotionalCore: Keen’s performed confidence meets a world too big to control, and the audience should feel the stomach-drop of being lost while still seeing him try to steady himself.
scenes.sceneShotName: Open sea — storm-front horizon, empty sailboat wide
scenes.definingFeature: the storm wall erasing the horizon ahead of the tiny sailboat
scenes.colorTemperature: cool storm blue-grey fear with fading midday blue underneath
scenes.cameraHeight: low waterline angle looking slightly up at the boat and towering storm sky
scenes.ambientBed: Open-water surf and wind, a steady hull-creak and slap of waves against the boat, gulls distant at first and gone once the storm rolls in.
scenes.parentLine: A parent watches a child perform confidence right up to the moment the world stops cooperating, then grounds himself with a small repeated promise instead of a rescue — the quiet lesson that courage is a breath, not a feeling of certainty.
scenes.sceneLook: Bright blue daylight drains into cold grey-blue storm light, visibility collapsing behind rain sheets.

scenes.name: Rainforest Edge Alarm
scenes.location: The wind-bent rainforest edge above Crystal Cove, where wet leaves and a low branch frame a screen-right opening out to the stormy sea.
scenes.time: Storm-dark afternoon, continuous with Keen being lost offshore.
scenes.weather: Wind rising hard through the trees with rain beginning to cut across the view toward the water.
scenes.lighting: Cool blue-grey storm light through shaking leaves, with the distant sea flattened by rain and low contrast.
scenes.look: Empty bee-height plate at the rainforest edge: soaked foreground leaves and a low branch cut across the screen-right flight path, with dark tree trunks and whipping foliage forming a tunnel toward a coastal gap. Through that gap, rain slants across a rough blue-grey sea; far beyond the trees, a tiny boat is barely visible rising and falling between waves. The frame is built for fast left-to-right bee movement, an abrupt mid-air stop, a branch bonk, and a clear sightline to the distant danger. No characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: The comedy snaps into concern when Fuzzby and Zenny see the danger, proving this world’s silliness carries real care when someone is alone in the storm.
scenes.sceneShotName: Rainforest edge — storm gap to distant boat, bee's-eye wide
scenes.definingFeature: the rain-lashed coastal gap framed by wet leaves, with the tiny boat barely visible beyond the trees
scenes.colorTemperature: cool storm-blue grey fear, motivated by storm clouds and rain over the water
scenes.cameraHeight: low bee's-eye at leaf-and-branch height, aimed screen-right through the rainforest gap
scenes.ambientBed: Hardening wind through rainforest canopy with rain beginning to lash the leaves, distant surf beyond the trees.
scenes.parentLine: A parent recognizes the exact moment a child's bravado gives way to real concern for someone else's safety, and how quickly imperfect help can still be the right help.
scenes.sceneLook: Cool blue-grey storm light through shaking leaves, with the distant sea flattened by rain and low contrast.

scenes.name: Crystal Cove Beacon
scenes.location: Crystal Cove beach at the wet shoreline, with the cove’s open water stretching screen-right into storm-dark distance.
scenes.time: Storm-dark afternoon, continuous with the alarm.
scenes.weather: Storm building over the cove with wind, rain and dark clouds gathering toward the open water.
scenes.lighting: Cold storm blue-grey dominates, broken by warm rose and multicolour crystal light that cuts outward like a rescue beacon.
scenes.look: An empty storm-lashed beach stage: wet sand and a shallow reflective shoreline dominate the foreground, with bent dune grasses at frame-left, scattered shells and one curved piece of driftwood placed along Fuzzby’s incoming crash path, and enough open sand at center for the bears’ circle. The sea fills the right half of frame, waves rising toward the horizon under gathering black rain clouds; rain streaks diagonally on the wind, and the far water remains visible enough for a tiny boat silhouette to later appear between waves. No characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: The cove becomes a circle of help before Keen can ask for it, and the audience should feel that being brave does not mean being unseen.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove shoreline — storm-dark beacon beach, low wide
scenes.definingFeature: the storm-dark open water framed by wet reflective sand, ready to become a crystal beacon path
scenes.colorTemperature: cool storm-blue grey uncertainty, motivated by rain clouds over the cove
scenes.cameraHeight: low shoreline height, slightly looking out across the wet sand toward the sea
scenes.ambientBed: Rising coastal storm: heavy surf against the shore, wind gusting through bent grasses, distant rolling thunder and steady rain hiss over the sea.
scenes.parentLine: A parent feels the whole arc of asking for help arrive in one breath — bravado covering fear, a friend finishing the sentence you can't, and a circle that acts together the instant it's needed.
scenes.sceneLook: Cold storm blue-grey dominates, broken by warm rose and multicolour crystal light that cuts outward like a rescue beacon.

scenes.name: Underwater Rescue
scenes.location: The storm-lashed open water just off Crystal Cove, with the rocky shoreline on one side, the small sailboat pitched in the wave field, and a submerged rock shelf beneath the churning surface.
scenes.time: Storm-dark afternoon at the emotional nadir.
scenes.weather: Full storm with crashing waves, heavy rain, current, foam and poor visibility.
scenes.lighting: Darkest point of the episode: cold underwater blue-grey and storm shadow, pierced by the distant beacon above and the first inner aqua glow from Keen’s vacant cuffs.
scenes.look: An empty storm rescue stage: black-blue waves dominate the frame, rain slants across heavy foam, the rocky shore sits frame-right for the watching point, the empty little sailboat pitches in the mid-distance frame-left, and a pale crystal beacon cuts through the grey from shore toward the sea. Beneath the nearest trough, the water briefly reveals the submerged rock and a ragged drift net snagged tight below the surface; no characters, no extra boats, no beach clutter.
scenes.emotionalCore: Keen is frightened, tired and nearly out of strength, yet he chooses Squeaky anyway, so the child feels courage as one more pull and the parent feels remembered love travelling through the cuffs.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove open sea — storm-dark rescue waters, low waterline wide
scenes.definingFeature: a cold crystal beacon spearing through storm rain across towering rescue waves
scenes.colorTemperature: cool storm-blue fear with a faint aquamarine crystal-beam hope
scenes.cameraHeight: low waterline angle looking across the waves toward the shore
scenes.ambientBed: Storm sea ambience: crashing waves, driving rain, wind gusting over water, distant thunder rolling underneath.
scenes.parentLine: A watching parent recognizes that courage is not the absence of fear or the ability to succeed alone, but choosing to act for someone else and then letting yourself be held when your own strength runs out.
scenes.sceneLook: Cold underwater blue-grey and storm shadow, pierced by the distant beacon above and the first inner aqua glow from Keen's vacant cuffs.

scenes.name: Washed Ashore Welcome
scenes.location: Crystal Cove beach at the shallow waterline: restless surf rolling in from screen-left, a broad stretch of wet reflective sand at centre for the welcome circle, and the sheltered crystal-cove beach rising behind.
scenes.time: Late afternoon as the storm begins to break.
scenes.weather: Rain easing, surf still restless but losing force, clouds starting to thin.
scenes.lighting: Cool storm light begins warming at the horizon; wet sand and crystals catch hopeful highlights.
scenes.look: Empty late-afternoon shoreline plate with churning but easing waves dominating the left and rear of frame, thin foam lines and ripples crossing the shallows, open wet sand in the centre foreground for Keen to land and be steadied, and a gentle crescent of dry sand behind where the group can gather. The cove’s glowing crystals and protective beach shelter sit in the background, warm enough to invite him in while the thinning storm clouds and unsettled sea still hold the distance to home. No characters, no extra props.
scenes.emotionalCore: Keen arrives shaken and alive into a new circle of care, and the audience should feel relief without erasing the distance from Mum.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove shallows — storm-break welcome, low wide shoreline plate
scenes.definingFeature: the reflective wet-sand waterline where storm surf meets the glowing crystal-sheltered cove
scenes.colorTemperature: storm-blue aquamarine warming toward late-afternoon gold — relief with the ache still present
scenes.cameraHeight: low shoreline height, slightly below bear eye-level looking across the shallows toward the shelter
scenes.ambientBed: Soft surf breathing against the shallows under a settling storm sky, with a light onshore breeze and the faint chime of cove crystals.
scenes.parentLine: A parent feels the whole arc of returning from danger here: the body is caught before the words come, the group makes room instead of crowding, and belonging is shown to protect rather than replace the bond back home.
scenes.sceneLook: Cool storm light begins warming at the horizon, wet sand and crystals catching hopeful highlights.

scenes.name: Aquamarine Homecoming
scenes.location: A sheltered post-storm gathering circle at Crystal Cove, just above the beach, with wet leaf-framed edges, smooth open ground at the centre, and crystal-veined cove stone enclosing the space.
scenes.time: Late afternoon after the storm has passed.
scenes.weather: Storm cleared, air fresh and washed clean with lingering droplets on leaves.
scenes.lighting: Warm hopeful post-storm sunlight with aquamarine crystal glow blooming at the center and reflecting in wet surfaces.
scenes.look: An empty ceremonial circle dominates the frame: clear open space in the middle for Keen and Aida to face each other, a broad ring of dry-enough ground around it for the full group to gather and then surge inward for a hug, wet leaves and lingering droplets framing the foreground, and subtle aquamarine crystals embedded around the cove wall and ground line catching the fresh late-afternoon light. The layout reads as a welcoming circle, not a stage; nothing blocks the centre, and the washed-clean air leaves room for a glowing crystal to appear between the two central positions.
scenes.emotionalCore: Keen receives the aquamarine not as a prize for fearlessness but as a welcome for choosing through fear, and the audience must feel that new family can honour the old one.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove — rain-washed aquamarine gathering circle, wide homecoming plate
scenes.definingFeature: the rain-washed circular cove clearing ringed by aquamarine-veined crystals
scenes.colorTemperature: warm late-afternoon amber through washed-clean air with a gentle aquamarine crystal undertone
scenes.cameraHeight: Keen eye-level, slightly low, looking into the open centre of the circle
scenes.ambientBed: Post-storm Crystal Cove hush: gentle dripping leaves, soft settling breeze, distant surf, faint crystal hum underneath
scenes.parentLine: A parent reads this as a child being seen and held by a new family without anyone asking him to let go of the one he lost.
scenes.sceneLook: Warm hopeful post-storm sunlight with aquamarine crystal glow blooming at the centre and reflecting in wet surfaces.

scenes.name: Honey Walk Ripple
scenes.location: Crystal Cove beach at the waterline, a clear walking stretch of rain-wet sand curving from frame-left surf toward the bright cove opening down screen-right.
scenes.time: Early evening golden hour, the same day settling after the storm.
scenes.weather: Clear and calm after rain, with softened air and gentle surf.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden-hour glow brighter and steadier than the morning, with aquamarine warmth lingering as an emotional trace.
scenes.look: An empty post-storm shoreline staged as a warm forward path: small calm waves lap in from frame-left, mirror-smooth wet sand dominates the foreground and midground for the group’s walk and reflections, and the beach opens gently toward screen-right into a peaceful glowing cove. Rain-polished leaves and crystal-veined rocks sparkle softly in the background; the frame stays uncluttered so Fuzzby can orbit beside Keen and Zenny can stop him cleanly without visual noise.
scenes.emotionalCore: Keen walks forward with a new family and the ache of home still inside him, so the final laugh lands as belonging rather than a reset.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove beach — post-storm golden-hour shoreline walk, low wide
scenes.definingFeature: the rain-polished golden wet-sand path curving beside gentle surf into the bright crystal cove
scenes.colorTemperature: amber golden-hour warmth after storm, safety with a saltwater ache
scenes.cameraHeight: low bear-eye level along the shoreline
scenes.ambientBed: Gentle post-storm waves lapping the shore, soft golden-hour breeze, distant birdsong settling
scenes.parentLine: A parent feels Keen being quietly absorbed into a new family through the safety of ordinary silliness, not a grand ceremony — belonging arriving as laughter, exactly when it's needed most.
scenes.sceneLook: Warm golden-hour glow brighter and steadier than the morning, with aquamarine warmth lingering as an emotional trace.

beats.storyBeat: The flower meadow opens alive with warm pollen and work-play rhythm as Fuzzby performs professional busyness, overdoes a pollen scoop, ricochets off a leaf and restores his dignity with a proud button.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should laugh immediately and feel the meadow as a safe, living playground before any storm exists.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to perform flawless pollen professionalism in front of Zenny.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to be loved as the messy, over-committed worker he actually is, not as the flawless expert he pretends to be.
beats.crystalTruth: No character crystal is present; the environmental crystals stay steady warm, while Fuzzby's proud face hides the need to be accepted after the FWIP.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby buzzes a funny work song, crashes into a leaf, and says he meant it.
beats.adultRead: The joke is a tiny rehearsal of the episode’s truth: confidence is often a performance laid over wobble.
beats.theGame: Pollen Professional: every flower counts only if Fuzzby can make it look official afterward.
beats.physicalFeeling: A bright stomach-lurch as the camera is pulled into Fuzzby’s speed in the first second.
beats.light: Warm amber morning shafts isolate the pollen path, with a gold glint catching the leaf just before the FWIP.
beats.atmosphere: Drifting pollen thickens around the flower corridor so each near-miss leaves a sparkling trail.
beats.motionTempo: Full-throttle from frame one: Fuzzby rockets, banks, dives, snaps back; Zenny glides and holds a precise working rhythm in the same frame.
beats.grade: Saturated golden-green meadow grade with honeyed highlights preserved after the crash button.

cuts.framing: 24mm wide-angle drone-chase at flower height, camera chases Fuzzby frame-left then banks around him while Zenny holds frame-right in the same moving world
cuts.action: Tall flowers sway in the warm breeze as Fuzzby rockets flower to flower collecting pollen, wingbeats rapid and continuous; Zenny glides between nearby blossoms with neat precision, her wingbeats rapid and continuous, while pollen loads bounce with each pass.
cuts.delivery: fun sing-song rhythm; proud work-song patter, breathless but delighted
cuts.voiceTreatment: voice forward over wing whirr; rhythmic with the wingbeats

cuts.framing: low 24mm chase-in, camera dives with Fuzzby toward an open flower then whips sideways to the leaf impact
cuts.action: Fuzzby dives low into the flower, scoops pollen, overdoes the exit, spins sideways into a leaf, hits FWIP, then bounces back into the air; his wings keep beating rapidly through the ricochet.

cuts.framing: 35mm quick push-in that snaps onto Fuzzby at eye level as he reclaims the frame; Zenny remains frame-right working in peripheral view
cuts.action: Fuzzby snaps upright in mid-air into a proud on-parade chest-out stance as if the FWIP was planned; Zenny holds her neat line beside him without giving him the laugh yet.
cuts.delivery: instant heroic cover-up; tiny breath after the line, absolutely no admission of failure
cuts.voiceTreatment: dry button, music ducks for the word

cuts.framing: 50mm locked-off short punctuation on Zenny frame-right, Fuzzby frame-left still posed at the edge of frame
cuts.action: Zenny glides up beside Fuzzby and holds on him for a beat, letting the silence frame his pride.

beats.cameraArc: The camera behaves like Fuzzby’s over-eager wingman: it chases, dives, whips with the crash, then spends its single held beat on Zenny’s locked punctuation.
beats.pauseHold: One held button under 1.5 seconds: Zenny’s flat watch after Fuzzby’s proud 'Nailed it.'

performance.surface: Fuzzby performs masterful competence; Zenny performs exact, dry restraint.
performance.underneath: Fuzzby knows the leaf won but refuses to let the moment know it.
performance.innerThought: Fuzzby: They saw nothing; I am magnificent. Zenny: I saw all of it.

beats.crystalGlow: No bear crystals present; ambient crystals steady warm gold, tiny pollen glints answering the comedy rather than signaling danger.
beats.startState: Fuzzby frame-left at flower height beside a pollen-heavy blossom, chest angled toward the next flower; Zenny frame-right beside a separate blossom, poised over her work line; towering flowers fill foreground and background in warm morning light.
beats.keyframePrompt: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film CGI (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush fur, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat. Deep within the rainforest flower meadow, as per the reference image of Fuzzby and as per the reference image of Zenny, Fuzzby frame-left at bee height beside a pollen-heavy blossom, Zenny frame-right beside another blossom, both placed in the start of a busy pollen run. Towering flowers with long necks, open petals, layered leaves, drifting pollen, and 2-3 subtle ambient crystals in soft bokeh. CINEMATOGRAPHY: Lens 24mm wide to make the flowers huge and the bees small; camera height at bee eye level; composition locks Fuzzby frame-left and Zenny frame-right with flower stems creating vertical scale; key light warm golden morning through canopy from upper-left, cool teal forest fill; shallow depth of field with foreground pollen sparkle; warm rim light motivated by sun through petals. Opening frame only, before the FWIP.
beats.i2vPrompt: Anchor Image is TRUTH. COPY EXACTLY. Multi-shot sequence with clean cuts between shots. Rule: any airborne bee has wings beating rapidly and continuously at all times. Shot 1 (24mm wide-angle drone-chase at flower height): Tall flowers sway in a warm breeze as Fuzzby rockets flower to flower collecting pollen, singing, FUZZBY: "BIZZY-BIZZY-BIZZY, BIZZY-BIZZY-BIZZY…" Zenny glides frame-right with neat precision in the same moving frame. Shot 2 (cut to low 24mm chase-in): Fuzzby dives low into a flower, scoops pollen, overdoes the exit, spins sideways into a leaf, FWIP, and bounces back into the air with visible weight. Shot 3 (cut to 35mm eye-level push-in): Fuzzby snaps upright into a proud on-parade chest-out stance and says, FUZZBY: "Nailed it." Shot 4 (cut to 50mm locked punctuation on Zenny): Zenny glides up beside him and holds on him for one dry beat; Fuzzby keeps the heroic stance at frame-left. cuts.action: The gathered bears turn together toward the storm-dark water as their separate glows gather into one shared brightness — wordless, complete.
cuts.delivery: Unified, warm and urgent, not shouted; every voice carries care more than force.

beats.cameraArc: The camera gathers individual lights into a circle, holds on the unified line, then releases toward the sea with the beacon so the scene exits on help traveling outward.
beats.pauseHold: Hold the final beacon path for about 1.4 seconds as rain parts around it and the storm sound opens toward the sea.

performance.surface: The group is focused, worried and hopeful; Aida anchors the call, and the others add themselves without hesitation.
performance.underneath: They are making a promise to someone they cannot yet reach: you are not alone in the big water.
performance.innerThought: All: Find him. Hold the path. Let our light reach before our paws can.

beats.storyBeat: The crystal beacon reaches the storm sea, Keen lifts with hope, then Squeaky is dragged under by the drift net and Keen chooses the water before he knows he can manage it.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience feels the stomach-drop from hope to danger, then the sharp inhale of a brave choice made with a gulp.
beats.want: Keen wants to keep proving he can handle the storm from the boat.
beats.need: Keen needs to let the danger be real and choose Squeaky anyway, not because he is fearless but because someone needs him.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen’s vacant wristbands remain mostly dark, holding the need underneath his brave face; the face performs readiness while the cuffs wait for the truth to be chosen.
beats.kidRead: The glowing beam looks magical, then Squeaky gets caught and Keen leaps into a giant storm rescue.
beats.adultRead: Keen’s courage begins before success: he swallows the size of the risk, then acts for someone else.
beats.theGame: Storm-Rescue Rules: when the sea shouts louder than your plan, the next right move is the smallest brave move you can make.
beats.physicalFeeling: A cold lurch in the belly as the boat bucks beneath him in the first seconds.
beats.light: Cold blue-grey storm light swallows the horizon while the distant crystal beam cuts a narrow aquamarine-white line across Keen’s face.
beats.atmosphere: Rain sheets, foam, and mist collapse the open sea into a tight pocket around the boat; the beacon particles shimmer against the grey before Squeaky’s cry snaps the air colder.
beats.motionTempo: Hope lifts for one breath, then the boat bucks, Keen gulps, squares, and leaps in one decisive acceleration.
beats.grade: Storm-blue low contrast with the beacon preserved as the only clean saturated line of hope.

beats.adultRead: Aida sees that the brave act was the choice to jump, not the outcome yet.
beats.theGame: Storm-Rescue Rules: the first pull does not have to win; it only has to keep you with the friend who is stuck.
beats.physicalFeeling: A held breath in the chest as the scene cuts from open storm noise to muffled underwater pressure.
beats.light: Cold shore light flattens the bears, then underwater blue-grey swallows detail; Aida’s steady rose warmth is the only held emotional light.
beats.atmosphere: Rain streaks on shore; underwater bubbles and silt drift sideways with the current, making the rescue feel heavy and close.

beats.want: Keen wants to force the rescue through effort alone.

beats.adultRead: The father-memory becomes support at the exact moment Keen cannot manufacture courage by himself.
beats.theGame: Storm-Rescue Rules: when the pull fails, the hidden helper is the love you remembered to bring with you.
beats.physicalFeeling: A slow squeeze in the chest as the sound drops and the water seems to press inward.

beats.motionTempo: Everything slows into weighted underwater effort: weak thrash, failed pull, upward glance, stilling, plant, center.

beats.emotionalIntent: Relief becomes bittersweet: Keen is safe in a new circle, but the ache of Mum remains present and honoured.
beats.want: Keen wants to understand the quiet exchange without letting the Mum-feeling fully show.
beats.need: He needs reassurance that home has not vanished just because he has arrived somewhere new.
beats.crystalTruth: Aida’s rose-quartz glow holds steady-warm while Keen’s vacant wristbands stay empty; the truth is not power yet, but connection — home can be reached, and he does not have to carry the distance alone.
beats.kidRead: Aida seems to speak dolphin with one small gesture, and Squeaky heads off with an important message.
beats.adultRead: The new community does not replace Mum; it protects the bond back to her, which is why Keen can begin to belong here without betrayal.
beats.theGame: Message-to-Mum Relay: the rule is the dolphin carries the worry home so Keen can stand safely in the new circle.
beats.physicalFeeling: A small lump-in-the-throat drop as the word Mum lands and Keen realises someone thought of her too.
beats.light: Rose-quartz warmth from Aida and the golden break in the clouds isolate Keen and the sea line, while the distant water stays cooler to keep the ache of distance alive.
beats.atmosphere: The rain has nearly stopped; mist hangs between beach and sea, and Squeaky’s final splash leaves rings that catch the returning light.
beats.motionTempo: Slow turn to sea, clean dolphin leap, quiet Aida lift, Squeaky dip-and-dive, then a still grateful hold.
beats.grade: Tender rose-amber over cool post-storm blue, preserving bittersweet distance inside the warmth.

cuts.framing: medium shoreline profile, 35mm, camera turns with Keen back toward the sea and holds Squeaky beyond the shallows
cuts.action: Keen turns back toward the sea; Squeaky leaps once more just beyond the shallows as Aida steps forward beside Keen.
cuts.delivery: curious and soft, still catching breath; the question carries a child’s trust before he understands.

cuts.framing: Aida and Keen two-shot, 50mm, camera holds steady as Aida lifts a small paw toward Squeaky
cuts.action: Aida holds a warm smile, lifts a paw in a small gesture to Squeaky, and Squeaky watches her, understands, dips her head once, then dives back into the waves.
cuts.dialogue: AIDA: Just getting a message home to your Mum. To let her know you’re okay.
cuts.delivery: soft, measured, practical tenderness; no grand magic voice, just care made useful.

cuts.framing: honest 50mm close on Keen, locked, with the sea soft behind him
cuts.action: Keen absorbs that, the sea behind him holding the distance to home as his face stills into a soft grateful pause.
cuts.delivery: small, sincere, almost whispered; the thanks is for Mum being remembered as much as for the message.

cuts.framing: quiet two-shot, 50mm, locked on Aida beside Keen with the circle softened in background
cuts.action: Aida gives Keen the smallest nod; the beach breathes around them and the last ripple from Squeaky spreads across the shallows.

beats.cameraArc: Turn from the welcome circle back to the sea, then still into Aida’s grounded tenderness and Keen’s grateful close.
beats.pauseHold: A 1.4 second grateful hold after KEEN: “Thank you.”, followed by Aida’s smallest nod as the emotional button.

performance.surface: Keen asks what Aida did and thanks her politely; Aida remains warm and composed.
performance.underneath: Keen’s Mum has rushed back into his heart, and Aida has protected that bond without making him ask.
performance.innerThought: Keen: Mum will know. I can be here and still belong to home. Aida: Let the message reach the person who had to let him go.

beats.storyBeat: The storm-cleared Crystal Cove gathers around Keen in a quiet circle; Aida names what he truly did, and aquamarine light begins to answer between them.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience feels the held breath of being truly seen after doing something hard, not praised for being fearless but welcomed for choosing through the storm.
beats.want: Keen wants to stand steady and receive the moment without showing how much the rescue and the goodbye still weigh inside him.
beats.need: Keen needs the circle to witness the whole truth: the gulp, the loss, the love from home, and the choice he made anyway.
beats.crystalTruth: The aquamarine shimmer brightens before Keen can fully smile, revealing the courage he carried when his face is still unsure; read: brightening from washed-clean quiet into steady aquamarine recognition.
beats.kidRead: Keen is in the middle of a glowing magic circle and a crystal is appearing just for him.
beats.adultRead: A child is being seen for the courage beneath the performance, and the new family honours the old love rather than replacing it.
beats.theGame: Circle of Welcome: the rule is that the light only appears when the circle sees the true choice, not the brave pose.
beats.physicalFeeling: a warm breath caught in the chest as the circle settles around Keen in the first two seconds
beats.light: Late-afternoon post-storm amber isolates Aida and Keen at the center while droplets catch small aquamarine glints before the crystal forms.
beats.atmosphere: Fresh washed air, sparkling leaf droplets and quiet crystal motes gather toward the center as if the cove is listening.
beats.motionTempo: Held and measured: the circle settles, Aida holds, Keen swallows and squares, then the light lifts into being.
beats.grade: Warm post-storm amber with aquamarine bloom preserved in the highlights; shadows stay soft blue to keep the storm’s memory present.

cuts.framing: wide establishing, low 35mm circle view, organic breathing push toward Aida and Keen at center

cuts.action: Aida stills the circle with one measured look; Keen swallows, then turns his paws slightly inward as the weight of being seen lands.
cuts.delivery: warm, measured, quiet authority; no announcement, more blessing than praise

cuts.framing: hook close-up, 85mm on the space between Aida and Keen, slow lift with the forming light
cuts.action: A gentle aquamarine light begins to shimmer in the air between them; a glowing crystal appears and hums, while Keen leans toward it in wonder.

beats.pauseHold: After Aida’s line, hold 1.2 seconds on Keen absorbing it before the aquamarine shimmer begins.

performance.surface: Keen stands upright and tries to receive Aida’s words like a brave new Crystal Bear.
performance.underneath: He is still wet from the storm inside, needing permission for the fear and the courage to exist together.
performance.innerThought: Did they really see me? Not the Yeehaw bit — the part where I almost couldn’t pull anymore?

Fuzzby snaps into a full-body GASPS take; his eyes sparkle with victory; Keen blinks through a tired smile. FUZZBY: "Oh… this is going to work out VERY well." Style+Audio: premium 3D CGI Pixar-quality animation, warm golden-hour post-rain beach, SFX and timed comedy music scored to the action, voice forward for lip-sync, soft surf, rapid bee wings, tiny comic chime on Fuzzby’s button, Keen aquamarine B note 493.88 Hz warm underneath. Duration 15 seconds. Ratio 16:9. No text, watermarks, logos.

beats.storyBeat: Zenny tries to rein Fuzzby in, but Fuzzby is already buzzing excitedly around Keen with a runaway honey list; Zenny catches one leg and pulls him back, and Fuzzby lands the final friendship button before everyone laughs and walks on.
beats.emotionalIntent: The final laugh lands as belonging: Keen is not being celebrated for being perfect, he is being claimed by a ridiculous friend and a patient circle.
beats.want: Fuzzby performs unstoppable enthusiasm, trying to seal the friendship with every honey idea at once; Zenny performs restraint so Keen is not overwhelmed.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to be allowed to love loudly without taking over; Zenny and the group need to make room for Keen at a pace his tired body can receive.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen’s aquamarine wristbands stay steady-warm-but-changed as he laughs: his face says the day is light again, but the crystal holds the truth that the ache of home and the memory of the storm still travel with him.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby zooms through a ridiculous honey menu until Zenny hauls him back by the leg.
beats.adultRead: Community is not just rescue and ceremony; it is someone noticing when welcome becomes too much and making it safe to keep walking.
beats.theGame: Honey Friendship Parade: Fuzzby believes every honey idea counts as friendship, while Zenny’s rule is one thing at a time.
beats.physicalFeeling: the buoyant little lift in the ribs when a room laughs with you instead of at you
beats.light: The setting sun widens across the beach for the final cove linger, while aquamarine undertones keep Keen connected to the rescue and the new family circle.
beats.atmosphere: Salt air softens, wing-buzz threads through warm surf, and wet leaves sparkle like the world is smiling but still damp from what happened.
beats.motionTempo: Fuzzby bursts into a tight three-part orbit, Zenny locks the rhythm with one clean catch, then the whole group settles into a shared forward walk.
beats.grade: Golden-hour amber with lingering aquamarine highlights, brighter and steadier than the morning but not sugary.

northStarAnswers.note: Best-effort package-level answer to the North Star test (CRYSTAL_BEARS_LOCKED_CANON.md §0). Canon states 4 test questions + 8 craft laws, not a literal 'six questions' — Julian's own definition of the exact six is still needed before this field can be more than 'answered honestly against what canon actually says' (cb_preflight.py's own flag).

testQuestions.laughOutLoud: Yes — Scene 1 (Fuzzby's pollen run, comedyMode=BIG on all 5 beats) and the Scene 3 pier comedy button (3.B6) are built as full slapstick set pieces; the wingman-chase camera register and the three-beat Flourish/Bumble/Cover-up engine carry this.
testQuestions.breatheIn: Yes, on two beats designed for it: 3.B4 (the cuff-fitting cheek brush, the scene's stated 'beauty breath') and 7.B3, the episode's one wordless held beat (wristband glow, 1.5s hold) at the story's nadir.
testQuestions.crystalTellsTruth: Checked across the crystal-bearing cast: Aida's rose quartz (6.B3/6.B4) brightens before her face changes, revealing need-for-connection beneath composed authority; Keen's aquamarine (9.B2) brightens while he is still stunned, revealing courage before he can perform it; the vacant wristbands (7.B3) brighten against his exhausted face. The bees (Fuzzby/Zenny) carry no crystal by design (1.B1's own crystalTruth states this explicitly) — their truth-telling runs through behavior/face only, never a crystal.
testQuestions.reachesKidAndParent: Kid layer: a bee's slapstick pollen run, a boy's storm adventure, a wristband that glows. Parent layer: per-scene parentLine fields (this pass) name the adult read scene by scene — a mother's practiced calm at a goodbye, the ache of watching a child choose risk, the relief that is also grief.

craftLaws.1_crystalIsNeedNotMood: Held — every crystalTruth field checked (1.B1, 6.B3, 6.B4, 7.B3, 9.B2) states the crystal CONTRADICTING the surface face, never agreeing with it.
craftLaws.2_wantVsNeedNamedEveryBeat: Held structurally — every beat already carries both a want and a need field (pre-existing in this package, verified present on all 43 beats).
craftLaws.3_surrenderNotPowerUp: Held, with one repair made this pass: 6.B3's line was changed from 'activate your crystal powers' (a power-up call) to 'let's give him our light' (a surrender/offering frame) during the Director's Eye fix pass.
craftLaws.4_oneWordlessHeldBeat: Confirmed exactly ONE beat carries wordlessHeld=true across the whole package: 7.B3, at the story's nadir (the underwater rescue's turn).
craftLaws.5_playIsTheVehicle: Present in at least one beat per its own 'theGame' field (e.g. 2.B1's 'Listening Bowl' game) — not audited beat-by-beat this pass; flagged as a good candidate for a future dedicated sweep rather than claimed complete here.
craftLaws.6_catchAndRelease: Addressed via each comedy beat's own pauseHold (a single named hold, capped ≤1.5s per this pass's fixes) and the comedyMode=BIG beats' three-beat comedy engine (flourish/bumble/cover-up) — not independently re-verified per beat this pass.
craftLaws.7_holdTheAcheBittersweet: Scene 10's closing beats (10.B1/10.B2) were authored this pass with this in mind (see their endState/parentLine) but this is a judgment call reserved for Julian's own eye, not self-certified here.
craftLaws.8_noteCarriesFeeling: Not audited this pass — each bear's canon musical note is a Gate-5/post-scoring concern more than a Gate-1 storyboard field; flagged, not claimed.

northStarAnswers.caveat: Presence/consistency-checked against existing fields where the data already supports it (want/need, crystalTruth, wordlessHeld); several items above are honestly flagged as NOT independently re-audited this pass rather than silently marked done. Whether the episode actually LANDS this test emotionally stays Julian's reserved showrunner verdict (rule 28) — this field documents structural evidence, not a quality verdict.
